project.name: SylphxAI Configs
project.summary: Shared configuration package monorepo for Sylphx TypeScript and Biome defaults.
goals: Provide the published @sylphx/tsconfig package for shared TypeScript configuration.
Provide the published @sylphx/biome-config package for shared Biome configuration.
Keep shared config packages small, versioned, and consumed through package exports rather than copied into downstream repositories.
nonGoals: Own downstream application code style exceptions or product-specific compiler behavior.
Own every repo's CI workflow, branch protection, or release process.
Own enterprise engineering doctrine, standards, or project manifest schema.

owns.name: typescript-config-package
owns.description: The @sylphx/tsconfig package, exported config files, README, changelog, and package metadata under packages/tsconfig.

owns.name: biome-config-package
owns.description: The @sylphx/biome-config package, shared Biome defaults, README, changelog, and package metadata under packages/biome-config.

doesNotOwn: Downstream application code style exceptions, product-specific compiler behavior, or repo-local lint policy deviations.
Every consumer repository's CI workflow, branch protection, release process, or package manager policy.
Enterprise doctrine and manifest schema owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema and engineering doctrine
ADR-29 admission action and reusable release workflow

forbiddenCouplings: Do not add product-specific compiler or formatter exceptions to shared config packages.
Do not make downstream repositories depend on unpublished internal config paths.
Do not fork doctrine standards into this repository.

delivery.deployPath: ADR-29 CI runs risk-classification/pass, trunk-admission/pass, postsubmit-proof/pass, and recovery-decision/pass around repo lint; main-branch Release delegates to SylphxAI/.github reusable release workflow for package publication.
delivery.productionProof: Passing lint/config validation, trunk admission fan-in, postsubmit proof on main, successful release workflow, and package-registry readback for published config versions.

gaps.description: ADR-29 workflow jobs exist, but branch-protection required contexts are not recorded in this manifest baseline.
Shared config changes should be validated against representative consumer repositories before production lifecycle remains credible at scale.
Published config package versions are forward-only for consumers; add bad-release recovery guidance before full adoption.
